refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall optimize the load→travel→dump cycle to meet or exceed the customer-defined cycle time target under specified duty cycle and site conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; refining it to focus on optimizing the cycle time clarifies the functional focus and enables targeted design solutions. This refinement aligns with the acceptance criteria and stakeholder expectations.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile cycle time (load→travel→dump) during representative duty cycles and site conditions and compare against the CT_target.
sub_frs: FR4: Coordinate cycle phases to reduce idle/wait times

candidate_dps: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms to optimize cycle phases
DP2: Real-time telemetry and tasking feedback loops
DP3: Integrated scheduling and coordination system
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Advanced vehicle control algorithms to optimize cycle phases

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and telemetry feedback
Coordination with safety services for operational constraints
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically focuses on vehicle control optimization, which can be modularized with clear interfaces to telemetry and safety services, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and timely telemetry data is available
Risk that vehicle control algorithms may conflict with safety constraints if not properly integrated
Assumes duty cycle and site conditions are well characterized
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to address telemetry feedback and scheduling explicitly.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and software components responsible for vehicle control and cycle phase optimization

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor data and sends vehicle status updates for cycle optimization

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands and cycle targets from higher-level coordination modules

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges safety status and alerts to ensure safe operation during cycle optimization

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as vehicle control algorithms are core to this module; low diffusion risk.
Broader scope; less focused containment for vehicle control.
Limited containment; vehicle control logic not primarily here.
Safety services provide constraints but do not contain vehicle control logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module is the best architectural boundary to contain the advanced vehicle control algorithms for cycle optimization, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Safety Services for real-time safety constraints
Risk of duplicated logic if telemetry feedback loops are not well modularized
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Safety Services and Teleoperation to mitigate diffusion risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (advanced vehicle control algorithms) directly addresses the refined FR of optimizing cycle time phases with clear logical mapping.

diffusion_risks: Possible overlap of telemetry processing between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Safety constraints integration may require cross-module coordination
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for DP; interfaces are well defined to limit diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring cycle time performance on the Vehicle module under controlled conditions and validating interface data flows.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of vehicle control DP to Vehicle IM with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Plan next-level decomposition to explicitly address telemetry and scheduling DPs.
Strengthen interface definitions with Safety Services to handle high safety criticality.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, the high safety criticality and telemetry integration require explicit consideration in the current level to reduce diffusion risks and clarify ownership.
next_level_sub_frs: FR4: Coordinate cycle phases to reduce idle/wait times

revise.why: To explicitly incorporate Safety Services and Teleoperation interfaces and responsibilities at this level to address safety criticality and telemetry integration.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely
Vehicle control algorithms can be modularized within Vehicle IM
open_risks: Potential diffusion of telemetry processing logic
evidence_needed: Interface performance and latency metrics between Vehicle and Safety Services
Telemetry data accuracy and update rates
